title: Problem with dollar signs in labels - `podman build --label 'test=$something' .`
body:
### Issue Description

Podman seems to handle labels with dollar signs different than Docker.

I am having the problem that I need to build an image that has a label with a dollar in it.
With Docker the dollar is treated as-is, which is desired-, but it seems Podman treats it like a variable or something, resulting in a blank label.
Have tried to escape with slashes or double dollars, but no such luck.


When using podman:


```
$ echo "FROM busybox" > Dockerfile
$ podman build -t test . --label 'test=$something'
STEP 1/2: FROM busybox
STEP 2/2: LABEL "test"="$something"
COMMIT test
--> e1d381555628
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
e1d38155562825539ff1da7eddef656dd7b4c9c86db642a0f8efa187a50852d5
$ docker inspect test
[...]
"Labels": {
"io.buildah.version": "1.43.1",
"test": ""
}
[...]
```


When using Docker:

```
$ echo "FROM busybox" > Dockerfile
$ docker build -t test --label 'test=$something' .

[+] Building 1.8s (5/5) FINISHED                                                                                                      docker:desktop-linux
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                                                                  0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 50B                                                                                                                   0.0s
 => [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/busybox:latest                                                                                     1.4s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                                                                     0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                                                                       0.0s
 => [1/1] FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest@sha256:fd8d9aa63ba2f0982b5304e1ee8d3b90a210bc1ffb5314d980eb6962f1a9715d                               0.2s
 => => resolve docker.io/library/busybox:latest@sha256:fd8d9aa63ba2f0982b5304e1ee8d3b90a210bc1ffb5314d980eb6962f1a9715d                               0.0s
 => => sha256:025fe1949698376d1d9a946f8a39a3529ad3ea540ca92b78c6cd041deb19d63e 1.92MB / 1.92MB                                                        0.2s
 => exporting to image                                                                                                                                0.3s
 => => exporting layers                                                                                                                               0.0s
 => => exporting manifest sha256:b15b925ce72e6758dda6a7f6588dc98585a8852d899bcb0416ef773e6d14ce51                                                     0.0s
 => => exporting config sha256:169ba401b1e475dc4dff5ccc58982ce025f3180c1c4c7c3e90ce07ebc31a46e2                                                       0.0s
 => => exporting attestation manifest sha256:ae1add153dc0b332f7f0b7459e6708a48f077288f3b77007294b53dfa81cd7d7                                         0.0s
 => => exporting manifest list sha256:3db12d787d4e9ecbe6d9678ce33323c72658d4f5690486c22ea400bdb7a523cd                                                0.0s
 => => naming to docker.io/library/test:latest                                                                                                        0.0s
 => => unpacking to docker.io/library/test:latest                                                                                                     0.3s
$ docker inspect test
[…]
            "Labels": {
                "test": "$something"
            },
[…]
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1) echo "FROM busybox" > Dockerfile
2) podman build -t test . --label 'test=$something'
3) docker inspect test


### Describe the results you received

"Labels": {
"io.buildah.version": "1.43.1",
"test": ""
}

### Describe the results you expected

Expected to see a label "test": "$something"


### podman info output

```yaml
podman version
Client: Podman Engine
Version: 5.8.1
API Version: 5.8.1
Go Version: go1.26.1
Git Commit: c6077f645788743258a1a749f8005b4fb3cbe533
Built: Wed Mar 11 20:57:57 2026
Build Origin: pkginstaller
OS/Arch: darwin/arm64

Server: Podman Engine
Version: 5.8.2
API Version: 5.8.2
Go Version: go1.25.9 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit: 5b263b5f5b48004a87caac44e67349a8266d9ef4
Built: Mon Apr 13 02:00:00 2026
OS/Arch: linux/arm64
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Using Podman desktop on a Mac

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting
